clusters.failureShape: A form input (search box, text field, combobox) has no visible, programmatically associated label and relies on a placeholder for its name, so the label disappears on input and is unreliable for assistive tech.
clusters.fix: Give every input a persistent visible <label> associated by id, or an aria-label when a visible label isn't possible. Never use placeholder text as the only label.

clusters.failureShape: An input with a format or length constraint (a 9-digit account number, a phone number split into single-digit boxes) provides no instructions or per-field labels, so users don't know the required format and error correction is hard.
clusters.fix: Provide visible instructions and constraints for the input (expected format, length), label each sub-field if the input is split, and prefer a single field that accepts the whole value to support autofill and easy correction.

clusters.failureShape: Two inputs share the same accessible name, or a label contains a typo, so screen-reader users can't distinguish the fields or get a confusing name.
clusters.fix: Give each input a unique, descriptive, correctly spelled label (e.g. "Origin city" vs "Destination city") so fields are distinguishable.

clusters.failureShape: A field's required (or optional) state isn't conveyed to assistive tech — a mandatory consent checkbox lacks a required indication — so users don't know which fields they must complete.
clusters.fix: Mark required fields with the required attribute / aria-required="true" and indicate the requirement in the visible label. Label optional fields explicitly when the default expectation is unclear.
